content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be redefined as layered, curiosity-pruned hierarchical memory architectures where the agent itself acts as a dynamic memory gatekeeper, rather than treating memory as a static storage module attached to the agent. This gatekeeping role leverages the agent’s intrinsic curiosity drive to prioritize which unlabeled experiences get encoded into long-term memory, rather than relying solely on task-specific performance metrics to select active learning samples.
